trans-unit.note: Source details text shown for packages from implicit channel or channels without Aspire* package source mappings

trans-unit.source: The Aspire CLI packages override directory '{0}' contains more than one version of the same Aspire package, which is ambiguous because the highest version would be selected silently. Clean the directory so each Aspire* package appears exactly once. Conflicts: {1}.
trans-unit.target: The Aspire CLI packages override directory '{0}' contains more than one version of the same Aspire package, which is ambiguous because the highest version would be selected silently. Clean the directory so each Aspire* package appears exactly once. Conflicts: {1}.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the packages override directory path. {1} is a semicolon-separated list of conflicting package ids with their versions, for example "Aspire.Hosting (13.4.1, 13.4.2)".

trans-unit.source: The Aspire CLI packages override directory '{0}' does not exist. Set ASPIRE_CLI_PACKAGES (or the install sidecar 'packages' field) to a directory that contains the .nupkg files to resolve Aspire* packages from.
trans-unit.target: The Aspire CLI packages override directory '{0}' does not exist. Set ASPIRE_CLI_PACKAGES (or the install sidecar 'packages' field) to a directory that contains the .nupkg files to resolve Aspire* packages from.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the configured packages override directory path. Shown when ASPIRE_CLI_PACKAGES or the sidecar 'packages' field points at a directory that is missing.

trans-unit.source: The 'staging' channel cannot be resolved from a CLI with identity '{0}'. Daily, local, and per-PR CLI builds do not have a deterministic staging feed. Install a staging or stable Aspire CLI, or set 'overrideStagingFeed' (for example: aspire config set -g overrideStagingFeed <feed-url>) to point at the staging feed you want to use.
trans-unit.target: The 'staging' channel cannot be resolved from a CLI with identity '{0}'. Daily, local, and per-PR CLI builds do not have a deterministic staging feed. Install a staging or stable Aspire CLI, or set 'overrideStagingFeed' (for example: aspire config set -g overrideStagingFeed <feed-url>) to point at the staging feed you want to use.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the running CLI's baked identity channel (one of daily, local, or pr-N). Shown when --channel staging is requested on a CLI that cannot produce a real staging feed. Tracks https://github.com/microsoft/aspire/issues/16652.
